title: Anulocaulis annulatus
intro: Anulocaulis annulatus, also known as ringstem, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Nyctaginaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Anulocaulis annulatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Anulocaulis annulatus is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, fever, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Anulocaulis annulatus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is also known as ringstem or ring-stemmed manzanita. Anulocaulis annulatus is a perennial herb that produces small white or pink flowers. It is often found in dry and sandy areas.
